words.definition_en: to carefully consider a situation, person or problem in order to make a judgment
words.example: It's not always easy to assess someone's suitability for a job just by talking to them.
words.example_vi: Không phải lúc nào cũng dễ dàng đánh giá sự phù hợp của ai đó cho một công việc chỉ qua việc trò chuyện với họ.

words.definition_en: to believe that something is true, even though no one has told you or even though you have no proof
words.example: I don't think you should presume that lots of people are going to help you.
words.example_vi: Tôi không nghĩ bạn nên mặc định rằng sẽ có nhiều người giúp đỡ mình đâu.

words.definition_en: if a problem, someone's behaviour, etc baffles you, you cannot understand it or solve it
words.example: Please don't baffle me with loads of technical terminology.
words.example_vi: Làm ơn đừng làm tôi rối tung lên với cả mớ thuật ngữ chuyên môn đó nữa.

words.definition_en: preferring one person, thing or idea to another in a way that is unfair
words.example: Most newspapers are usually biased towards a certain political outlook.
words.example_vi: Hầu hết các tờ báo thường có xu hướng thiên vị một quan điểm chính trị nhất định.

words.definition_en: to give all your attention to the thing you are doing
words.example: You really have to concentrate very hard when you are doing calligraphy.
words.example_vi: Bạn thực sự phải tập trung cao độ khi viết thư pháp.

words.definition_en: to think about something carefully before making a decision or developing an opinion
words.example: I'm seriously considering asking for a pay rise tomorrow.
words.example_vi: Tôi đang nghiêm túc cân nhắc việc xin tăng lương vào ngày mai.

words.definition_en: to consider doing something in the future
words.example: Let's not even contemplate the possibility of failure.
words.example_vi: Đừng thậm chí nghĩ đến khả năng thất bại.

words.definition_en: believing that people are only interested in themselves and are not sincere or honest
words.definition_vi: hoài nghi (theo hướng tiêu cực, cho rằng ai cũng ích kỷ)
words.example: I'm slightly dubious about whether Sean's idea is going to work.
words.example_vi: Tôi hơi nghi ngờ về việc liệu ý tưởng của Sean có thành công hay không.

words.definition_en: to know something as a result of considering the information or evidence that you have
words.example: Looking at the damage, I would deduce that the painting will cost at least €2,000 to restore.
words.example_vi: Nhìn vào những hư hại, tôi suy luận rằng bức tranh sẽ tốn ít nhất 2.000 euro để phục chế.

words.definition_en: to think about or discuss something very carefully, especially before you make an important decision
words.definition_vi: thảo luận kỹ lưỡng, cân nhắc
words.example: The jury are still deliberating, but they're expected to reach a verdict soon.
words.example_vi: Ban bồi thẩm vẫn đang thảo luận kỹ lưỡng, nhưng họ dự kiến sẽ sớm đưa ra phán quyết.

words.definition_en: a situation in which you have to make a difficult decision
words.definition_vi: tình thế tiến thoái lưỡng nan
words.example: I'm in a real dilemma because I've been invited to two parties.
words.example_vi: Tôi đang ở trong một tình thế tiến thoái lưỡng nan thực sự vì được mời đi tận hai bữa tiệc.

words.definition_en: to treat someone unfairly because of their religion, race or other personal features
words.example: Employers are not allowed to discriminate on the basis of gender.
words.example_vi: Chủ lao động không được phép phân biệt đối xử dựa trên giới tính.

words.definition_en: not completely good, safe or honest; doubting that something is true or useful
words.definition_vi: mơ hồ, đáng ngờ, hoài nghi
words.example: I'm slightly dubious about whether this plan will work.
words.example_vi: Tôi hơi hoài nghi về việc liệu kế hoạch này có hiệu quả hay không.

words.definition_en: to guess or calculate an amount or value by using available information
words.example: I would estimate that the project will take six months.
words.example_vi: Tôi ước tính rằng dự án này sẽ mất khoảng sáu tháng.

words.definition_en: strong belief in or trust of someone or something
words.example: Dave said he's got complete faith in us.
words.example_vi: Dave nói rằng anh ấy hoàn toàn tin tưởng vào chúng ta.

words.definition_en: to believe that something is true, although no one has directly told you so
words.definition_vi: hiểu, nắm bắt được thông tin (qua nghe ngóng)
words.example: I gather from your tutor that you're thinking of changing course.
words.example_vi: Qua lời gia sư của bạn, tôi hiểu rằng bạn đang có ý định đổi khóa học.

words.definition_en: someone who is much more intelligent or skilful than other people
words.example: Sarah's a genius when it comes to astrophysics.
words.example_vi: Sarah là một thiên tài khi nói đến lĩnh vực vật lý thiên văn.

words.definition_en: to understand something
words.example: It took me quite a long time to grasp what the aim of the game was.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã mất khá nhiều thời gian mới nắm bắt được mục tiêu của trò chơi này là gì.

words.definition_en: the process of trying to find the answer to something by guessing, or the answer found by using this method
words.example: You often need to use a little educated guesswork when you're starting a cryptic crossword.
words.example_vi: Bạn thường cần sử dụng một chút phán đoán có cơ sở khi bắt đầu giải một trò chơi ô chữ hóc búa.

words.definition_en: a feeling that something is true or will happen, although you do not know any definite facts about it
words.example: I've got a hunch that someone in this office is going to get promoted.
words.example_vi: Tôi có linh tính rằng ai đó trong văn phòng này sắp được thăng chức.

words.definition_en: a set of ideas and beliefs on which a political or economic system is based
words.example: Communism and capitalism are very different types of ideology.
words.example_vi: Chủ nghĩa cộng sản và chủ nghĩa tư bản là những loại hình hệ tư tưởng rất khác nhau.

words.definition_en: an ingenious plan, piece of equipment, etc uses new and clever ideas
words.example: The only ingenious explanation I can think of is that Jenna got the dates wrong.
words.example_vi: Giải thích khéo léo duy nhất mà tôi có thể nghĩ ra là Jenna đã nhầm ngày tháng.

words.definition_en: suddenly having an idea about what you should do or create
words.example: Who gave you the inspiration for the main character in the book?
words.example_vi: Ai đã truyền cảm hứng cho bạn để xây dựng nhân vật chính trong cuốn sách này vậy?

words.definition_en: an ability to know or understand something through your feelings, rather than by considering facts or evidence
words.example: All his intuition told him that he was involved.
words.example_vi: Tất cả trực giác đều mách bảo anh rằng ông ta có liên quan.

words.definition_en: to show that there is a good reason for something, especially something that other people think is wrong
words.example: How can the government justify spending so much on the armed forces?
words.example_vi: Làm sao chính phủ có thể biện minh cho việc chi tiêu quá nhiều vào lực lượng vũ trang?

words.definition_en: a naive person lacks experience of life and tends to trust other people and believe things too easily
words.example: It's a little naive to suggest that it's possible to create a society without crime.
words.example_vi: Có chút ngây thơ khi cho rằng có thể tạo ra một xã hội không có tội phạm.

words.definition_en: an idea or belief
words.example: Paying mothers a salary to raise their children is an interesting notion.
words.example_vi: Trả lương cho những người mẹ để nuôi dạy con cái là một ý tưởng thú vị.

words.definition_en: someone who is optimistic is hopeful about the future and tends to expect that good things will happen
words.example: Everything's going to be fine, you'll see! Don't be so optimistic.
words.example_vi: Mọi chuyện rồi sẽ ổn thôi, bạn xem này! Đừng có quá lạc quan như vậy.

words.definition_en: a person, thing or situation that is strange because they have features or qualities that do not normally exist together
words.example: The fact that more people would come here if the place wasn't so crowded is a real paradox.
words.example_vi: Việc có nhiều người sẽ đến đây hơn nếu nơi này không quá đông đúc là một nghịch lý thực sự.

words.definition_en: someone who is pessimistic is not hopeful about the future and tends to expect that bad things will happen
words.example: Don't be so pessimistic. Everything's going to be fine.
words.example_vi: Đừng bi quan quá. Mọi chuyện rồi sẽ ổn thôi.

words.definition_en: likely to be true, honest or suitable
words.example: The only plausible explanation I can think of is that Jenna got the dates wrong.
words.example_vi: Giải thích hợp lý duy nhất mà tôi có thể nghĩ ra là Jenna đã nhầm ngày tháng.

words.definition_en: to think carefully about something for a long time before reaching a decision
words.example: If you've ever pondered the question 'Why does the universe bother to exist?'
words.example_vi: Nếu bạn đã từng suy ngẫm về câu hỏi 'Tại sao vũ trụ lại tồn tại làm gì?'

words.definition_en: someone who is prejudiced has an unreasonable opinion or feeling about someone or something, especially hatred or fear of a particular group of people
words.example: Most newspapers are usually biased towards a certain political outlook.
words.example_vi: Hầu hết các tờ báo thường có định kiến thiên về một quan điểm chính trị nhất định.

words.definition_en: to think that something is true because it is likely, although you cannot be certain
words.example: I don't think you should presume that lots of people are going to help you.
words.example_vi: Tôi không nghĩ bạn nên tự phụ rằng sẽ có nhiều người giúp đỡ mình đâu.

words.definition_en: a question that you ask because you want information or because you are not certain about something
words.example: I've got a small query about the exam syllabus.
words.example_vi: Tôi có một thắc mắc nhỏ về đề cương ôn thi.

words.definition_en: to believe that something is true
words.example: How long do you reckon it'll take to get to Manchester?
words.example_vi: Bạn tính xem mất bao lâu để đến được Manchester?

words.definition_en: to think about something carefully and seriously
words.example: Josie reflected on how easily she could have been killed.
words.example_vi: Josie ngẫm lại việc cô ấy đã có thể mất mạng dễ dàng như thế nào.

words.definition_en: having doubts about something that other people think is true or right
words.example: Harrison was extremely sceptical about the whole idea.
words.example_vi: Harrison cực kỳ hoài nghi về toàn bộ ý tưởng này.

words.definition_en: to consider or discuss why something has happened
words.example: We can only speculate on the reasons for his decision.
words.example_vi: Chúng ta chỉ có thể suy đoán về lý do đằng sau quyết định của anh ấy.

words.definition_en: to believe that something is probably true, based on your experience, your knowledge and any other information that you have
words.example: I don't suppose I could borrow your lecture notes, could I?
words.example_vi: Tôi đoán là tôi không thể mượn vở ghi bài của bạn được đâu nhỉ?

words.definition_en: relating to education, especially in colleges and universities
words.example: The standard curriculum doesn't give students any tuition in developing practical skills.
words.example_vi: Chương trình giảng dạy chuẩn không cung cấp cho sinh viên bất kỳ sự hướng dẫn nào trong việc phát triển các kỹ năng thực tế.

words.definition_en: working hard and being careful to do things well
words.example: The brightest and most conscientious students are not encouraged to develop to their full potential.
words.example_vi: Những sinh viên sáng giá và tận tâm nhất lại không được khuyến khích phát triển hết tiềm năng của mình.

words.definition_en: to study hard in a short period of time before an exam
words.example: My daughter has just spent the last month cramming for exams.
words.example_vi: Con gái tôi vừa dành cả tháng qua để học nhồi nhét cho kỳ thi.

words.definition_en: the subjects that students study at a particular school or college
words.example: The standard curriculum doesn't give students any tuition in developing practical skills.
words.example_vi: Chương trình học chuẩn không dạy cho học sinh bất kỳ kỹ năng thực hành nào.

words.definition_en: a system in which students study at home with the help of television and radio broadcasts and send work to their teachers by post or email
words.example: Distance learning is becoming increasingly popular.
words.example_vi: Học từ xa đang ngày càng trở nên phổ biến.

words.definition_en: someone who has a degree from a university
words.example: How many students graduate from university totally unable to spell?
words.example_vi: Có bao nhiêu sinh viên tốt nghiệp đại học mà hoàn toàn không biết viết đúng chính tả nhỉ?

words.definition_en: not knowing something that you should know
words.example: I was ignorant of the fact that it was a holiday.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã không hề biết rằng hôm đó là ngày lễ.

words.definition_en: not giving much attention to someone or something
words.example: He was sometimes inattentive in class.
words.example_vi: Đôi khi anh ấy lơ đãng trong giờ học.

words.definition_en: relating to the ability to think in an intelligent way and to understand complex ideas
words.definition_vi: thuộc trí tuệ, người trí thức
words.example: He's very intellectual and enjoys discussing philosophy.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy là người rất trí thức và thích thảo luận về triết học.

words.definition_en: good at thinking clearly and quickly, at understanding difficult ideas and subjects, and at gaining and using knowledge
words.example: She's a very intelligent young woman.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy là một phụ nữ trẻ rất thông minh.

words.definition_en: involving a lot of teaching or training in a short period of time
words.example: I did an intensive course in German.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã tham gia một khóa học tiếng Đức cấp tốc.

words.definition_en: knowing a lot about many different subjects or about one particular subject
words.example: Is the work she's been doing really going to make her more knowledgeable about her subjects?
words.example_vi: Liệu công việc cô ấy đang làm có thực sự giúp cô ấy am hiểu hơn về các môn học của mình không?

words.definition_en: a talk to a group of people about a particular subject, especially at a college or university
words.example: I don't suppose I could borrow your lecture notes this weekend.
words.example_vi: Chắc là tôi không mượn được vở ghi bài của bạn cuối tuần này đâu nhỉ.

words.definition_en: an examination you take for practice before an important examination
words.example: They're just mock exams. The real GCSEs will be next year.
words.example_vi: Đó chỉ là các kỳ thi thử thôi. Kỳ thi GCSE chính thức sẽ diễn ra vào năm tới.

words.definition_en: to take someone else's work, ideas or words and use them as if they were your own
words.example: How many students enter university with no idea of the difference between plagiarising and making good use of ideas?
words.example_vi: Có bao nhiêu sinh viên vào đại học mà không hề biết sự khác biệt giữa đạo văn và việc sử dụng tốt các ý tưởng?

words.definition_en: work that you do without the help of a teacher
words.example: How many of them are really able to go about self-study in an efficient way?
words.example_vi: Có bao nhiêu người trong số họ thực sự có khả năng tự học một cách hiệu quả?

words.definition_en: a class at a college or university in which a small group of students discuss a subject with a teacher
words.example: We had a very interesting seminar on history today.
words.example_vi: Hôm nay chúng tôi đã có một buổi hội thảo rất thú vị về lịch sử.

words.definition_en: the particular needs of people who are physically or mentally disabled
words.definition_vi: nhu cầu đặc biệt (cho người khuyết tật)
words.example: The system only views the weakest learners as having special needs.
words.example_vi: Hệ thống này chỉ xem những người học yếu nhất là đối tượng có nhu cầu đặc biệt.

words.definition_en: the work that a teacher does when they teach a particular subject, especially to one person or a small group
words.example: The standard curriculum doesn't give students any tuition in developing practical skills.
words.example_vi: Chương trình học chuẩn không cung cấp sự chỉ dẫn nào về phát triển kỹ năng thực hành cho học sinh.

words.definition_en: a lesson in which a small group of students discuss a subject with a tutor, especially at university or college
words.definition_vi: buổi hướng dẫn, thảo luận nhóm nhỏ
words.example: We had a tutorial on the French Revolution.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã có một buổi hướng dẫn về Cách mạng Pháp.

words.definition_en: practise and improve your skills or knowledge of something
words.example: I'm going to have to brush up on my spoken German before I go to Munich.
words.example_vi: Tôi sẽ phải ôn lại kỹ năng nói tiếng Đức của mình trước khi đi Munich.

words.definition_en: change your opinion or decision because someone has persuaded you to agree with them
words.definition_vi: thay đổi ý kiến (bị thuyết phục)
words.example: I didn't like the idea at first but I've come round to it now.
words.example_vi: Lúc đầu tôi không thích ý tưởng này nhưng giờ tôi đã dần chấp nhận nó rồi.

words.definition_en: think of something such as an idea or a plan
words.example: Lee came up with the brilliant idea of booking a clown.
words.example_vi: Lee đã nảy ra ý tưởng tuyệt vời là thuê một chú hề.

words.definition_en: accept something and try to deal with it
words.example: It's time you faced up to the fact that you've got responsibilities now.
words.example_vi: Đã đến lúc bạn phải đối mặt với sự thật rằng bây giờ bạn đã có những trách nhiệm riêng rồi.

words.definition_en: be able to understand something or solve a problem; understand what someone is like and why they behave in the way they do
words.example: It took us ages to figure out how to get everyone to the sports centre.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã mất rất nhiều thời gian mới tìm ra cách đưa mọi người đến trung tâm thể thao.

words.definition_en: suddenly have an idea; discover something by chance
words.definition_vi: nảy ra ý tưởng, tình cờ phát hiện
words.example: Tell us how you hit upon such a great idea for a website.
words.example_vi: Hãy kể cho chúng tôi nghe bạn đã tình cờ nghĩ ra ý tưởng tuyệt vời cho trang web đó như thế nào đi.

words.definition_en: see, hear or understand someone or something with difficulty; suggest, imply
words.definition_vi: nhìn/nghe/hiểu một cách khó khăn; giả vờ/quả quyết
words.example: I was unable to make out what the speaker was saying.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã không thể nghe ra được người nói đang nói gì.

words.definition_en: think carefully about something over a period of time
words.example: I need to mull over your offer very carefully before I make a decision.
words.example_vi: Tôi cần cân nhắc lời đề nghị của bạn thật kỹ trước khi đưa ra quyết định.

words.definition_en: learn the truth about something by considering all the separate bits of information you have
words.example: Detectives are still trying to piece together exactly what happened that evening.
words.example_vi: Các thám tử vẫn đang cố gắng xâu chuỗi lại chính xác những gì đã xảy ra vào tối hôm đó.

words.definition_en: solve a confusing or complicated problem by thinking carefully about it
words.definition_vi: giải mã, tìm ra lời giải
words.example: We finally puzzled out a way to get the band into the hotel.
words.example_vi: Cuối cùng chúng tôi cũng tìm ra cách để đưa ban nhạc vào khách sạn.

words.definition_en: get information on a particular subject by reading a lot about it
words.definition_vi: tìm hiểu kỹ qua việc đọc
words.example: I've been reading up on the history of English language teaching recently.
words.example_vi: Gần đây tôi đang tìm đọc thêm về lịch sử giảng dạy tiếng Anh.

words.definition_en: study something very hard, especially for an examination
words.example: There's nothing to swot up on as it's a general knowledge quiz.
words.example_vi: Không có gì cần phải học gạo cả vì đây là một cuộc thi đố kiến thức tổng quát thôi.

words.definition_en: understand and remember something that you hear or read; accept something as real or true; trick someone into believing something that is not true
words.definition_vi: hấp thụ kiến thức; lừa gạt; chấp nhận
words.example: It took him a very long time to take in the fact that he'd never see her again.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã mất một thời gian rất dài để chấp nhận sự thật rằng mình sẽ không bao giờ được gặp lại cô ấy nữa.

words.definition_en: consider a problem or decision carefully
words.example: I'm going to have to think this over for a while before I make a final decision.
words.example_vi: Tôi sẽ phải suy nghĩ kỹ về việc này một thời gian trước khi đưa ra quyết định cuối cùng.

words.definition_en: consider the facts about something in an organised and thorough way
words.definition_vi: suy nghĩ thông suốt, kỹ lưỡng
words.example: I'm not sure we've thought this through properly yet.
words.example_vi: Tôi không chắc là chúng ta đã suy tính thấu đáo việc này chưa nữa.

words.definition_en: invent or imagine something, especially an excuse
words.example: I've just thought up a brilliant excuse to tell Rachel.
words.example_vi: Tôi vừa mới nghĩ ra một lý do thoái thác cực hay để nói với Rachel.

words.definition_en: a written or spoken description of an event
words.definition_vi: bản kê khai, sự giải thích
words.example: Could you give an account of your whereabouts last night?
words.example_vi: Bạn có thể giải trình về việc bạn đã ở đâu tối qua không?

words.definition_en: to make a connection in your mind between one person or thing and another
words.example: I think I'll always associate this song with our holiday in Majorca.
words.example_vi: Tôi nghĩ mình sẽ luôn gắn liền bài hát này với kỳ nghỉ của chúng ta ở Majorca.

words.definition_en: a state in which different things are of equal importance or are correct in relation to each other
words.example: You have to strike a balance between logic and lateral thinking.
words.example_vi: Bạn phải giữ được sự cân bằng giữa tư duy logic và tư duy sáng tạo.

words.definition_en: the facts, ideas, or things from which something can be developed
words.example: You were hired on the basis that you would be able to work on Sundays.
words.example_vi: Bạn được thuê dựa trên cơ sở là bạn có thể làm việc vào các ngày Chủ nhật.

words.definition_en: a feeling that something is true or right
words.example: Where did Susan's belief in reincarnation come from?
words.example_vi: Niềm tin vào sự luân hồi của Susan bắt nguồn từ đâu vậy?

words.definition_en: the organ inside your head that allows you to think and feel
words.example: Could I just pick your brains about good places to eat?
words.example_vi: Tôi có thể hỏi ý kiến bạn một chút về những địa điểm ăn uống ngon không?

words.definition_en: something that you decide after considering all the information you have
words.example: In conclusion, I'd like to thank everyone who supported me.
words.example_vi: Để kết luận, tôi muốn gửi lời cảm ơn đến tất cả những người đã ủng hộ tôi.

words.definition_en: careful thought before making a decision
words.definition_vi: sự cân nhắc, sự chu đáo
words.example: I'll give some consideration to your proposals.
words.example_vi: Tôi sẽ xem xét các đề xuất của bạn.

words.definition_en: a feeling of being not sure whether something is true or right
words.example: These findings cast doubt on previous research.
words.example_vi: Những phát hiện này làm dấy lên nghi ngờ về các nghiên cứu trước đây.

words.definition_en: something that you want to happen very much
words.example: Winning a cruise was a dream come true for me.
words.example_vi: Giành được một chuyến du hành trên biển là một giấc mơ có thật đối với tôi.

words.definition_en: the thing that people are concentrating on or the most important part of something
words.example: This photo's a little out of focus.
words.example_vi: Bức ảnh này hơi bị mờ một chút.

words.definition_en: the opinion or feeling you have about someone or something because of the way they seem
words.example: Why was I under the impression that the exam was on Thursday?
words.example_vi: Tại sao tôi lại cứ đinh ninh rằng kỳ thi diễn ra vào thứ Năm nhỉ?

words.definition_en: relating to the mind and thinking
words.definition_vi: thuộc về tinh thần, trí tuệ
words.example: Many mental illnesses can be cured these days.
words.example_vi: Ngày nay có nhiều bệnh tâm thần có thể chữa khỏi được.

words.definition_en: your thoughts, or your ability to think
words.example: It never crossed my mind that we'd have legal problems.
words.example_vi: Tôi chưa bao giờ mảy may nghĩ đến việc chúng ta sẽ gặp rắc rối về pháp lý.

words.definition_en: a wrong idea about something
words.example: Jim seems to be under the misapprehension that tomorrow's a holiday.
words.example_vi: Jim có vẻ như đang hiểu lầm rằng ngày mai là ngày lễ.

words.definition_en: a way of thinking about something
words.example: Hearing the news put my small problems into perspective.
words.example_vi: Khi nghe tin tức đó, tôi thấy những rắc rối nhỏ nhặt của mình chẳng đáng là bao.

words.definition_en: a basic belief, theory, or rule that has a major influence on the way in which something is done
words.example: I'm not against animal testing in principle.
words.example_vi: Về nguyên tắc, tôi không phản đối việc thử nghiệm trên động vật.

words.definition_en: a doubt about something
words.example: Dad said that my borrowing the car tonight was out of the question.
words.example_vi: Bố nói rằng việc tôi mượn xe tối nay là điều không thể.

words.definition_en: a feeling about something
words.example: Unplugging a toaster before you fix it is just common sense.
words.example_vi: Rút phích cắm lò nướng bánh trước khi sửa nó chỉ là lẽ thường tình thôi.

words.definition_en: one of the people, groups, or countries involved in an argument
words.example: Look on the bright side - it's nearly the end of term.
words.example_vi: Hãy nhìn vào mặt tích cực đi - sắp kết thúc học kỳ rồi mà.

words.definition_en: not bending or curving; honest and direct
words.example: I'll come straight to the point - you're fired!
words.example_vi: Tôi sẽ đi thẳng vào vấn đề luôn - bạn đã bị đuổi việc!

words.definition_en: your opinion about something
words.example: What's the best course of action from your point of view?
words.example_vi: Theo quan điểm của bạn, đâu là phương án hành động tốt nhất?

words.definition_en: if success goes to your head, it makes you think that you are better or more important than you really are
words.definition_vi: làm cho ai đó kiêu ngạo
words.example: Don't let success go to your head, will you?
words.example_vi: Đừng để thành công làm bạn ảo tưởng về bản thân nhé?

words.definition_en: be able to think quickly and make sensible decisions
words.definition_vi: giữ được sự bình tĩnh và sáng suốt
words.example: You've got to have your wits about you to succeed in advertising.
words.example_vi: Bạn phải cực kỳ nhạy bén để thành công trong ngành quảng cáo.

words.definition_en: not knowing very much about something, because other people are keeping it secret from you
words.definition_vi: không được biết, bị giữ kín thông tin
words.example: How did they manage to keep me completely in the dark about this for so long?
words.example_vi: Làm sao họ có thể giấu nhẹm chuyện này với tôi trong suốt một thời gian dài như vậy?

words.definition_en: know the important facts about a situation
words.definition_vi: biết rõ điều gì là quan trọng, thạo việc
words.example: Ask Simon about the sales targets. He always knows what's what.
words.example_vi: Hãy hỏi Simon về các mục tiêu doanh số. Anh ấy luôn là người am hiểu mọi việc.

words.word: not have a leg to stand on
words.definition_en: not have any way of proving that you are right about something
words.definition_vi: không có lý lẽ để bào chữa
words.example: Frankly, you're completely wrong and you haven't got a leg to stand on.
words.example_vi: Thẳng thắn mà nói, bạn hoàn toàn sai rồi và bạn chẳng có chút lý lẽ nào để tự bào chữa cả.

words.word: not see the wood for the trees
words.definition_en: used for saying that someone cannot understand what is important in a situation because they are thinking too much about small details
words.definition_vi: không thấy được toàn cảnh vì quá chú trọng chi tiết
words.example: The problem with Jane is that sometimes she can't see the wood for the trees.
words.example_vi: Vấn đề của Jane là đôi khi cô ấy quá sa đà vào chi tiết mà quên mất cái nhìn tổng thể.

words.definition_en: guess what is happening or what something means based on what you have seen or heard
words.definition_vi: cứ thế mà suy ra (dựa trên bằng chứng có sẵn)
words.example: No one told us that Diana was leaving, but we all put two and two together.
words.example_vi: Không ai nói với chúng tôi rằng Diana sẽ rời đi, nhưng tất cả chúng tôi đều tự suy luận ra được.

words.definition_en: taking a very short/long time to understand or realise something
words.example: How did Dan take so long? He's usually quicker on the uptake than that.
words.example_vi: Sao Dan lại mất nhiều thời gian đến vậy nhỉ? Thường thì anh ấy tiếp thu nhanh hơn thế nhiều.

words.definition_en: something that rings a bell sounds familiar to you, although you cannot remember the exact details
words.example: The name Alan Hickson rings a bell, but I can't place him.
words.example_vi: Cái tên Alan Hickson nghe quen lắm, nhưng tôi không thể nhớ ra ông ấy là ai.

words.definition_en: crazy
words.example: I'm going to go round the bend if they don't turn that music down soon.
words.example_vi: Tôi sắp phát điên lên mất nếu họ không vặn nhỏ nhạc xuống sớm.

words.definition_en: argue or worry about very small details or differences that are not important
words.definition_vi: bới lông tìm vết, so đo những điều vụn vặt
words.example: Stop wasting time splitting hairs and look at the big picture.
words.example_vi: Đừng lãng phí thời gian vào việc bắt bẻ vụn vặt nữa mà hãy nhìn vào bức tranh toàn cảnh đi.

words.definition_en: spend some time thinking about the situation you are in before you decide what to do next
words.definition_vi: xem xét, đánh giá lại tình hình
words.example: Let's take stock of the situation before we make a final decision.
words.example_vi: Hãy xem xét đánh giá lại tình hình trước khi chúng ta đưa ra quyết định cuối cùng.

items.sentence: Don't be so ___ / pessimistic. Everything's going to be fine, you'll see!
items.explanation: The speaker is encouraging a positive outlook ('Everything's going to be fine').

items.sentence: Most newspapers are usually ___ / prejudiced towards a certain political outlook.
items.explanation: 'Biased' is commonly used to describe media leaning towards a specific viewpoint.

items.sentence: I'm slightly ___ / cynical about whether Sean's idea is going to work.
items.explanation: 'Dubious' means feeling doubt or uncertainty about something.

items.sentence: To suggest that it's possible to create a society without crime is a little ___ / sceptical, isn't it?
items.explanation: Suggesting an impossible ideal society is 'naive' (lacking experience/wisdom).

items.sentence: The only ___ / plausible explanation I can think of is that Jenna got the dates wrong.
items.explanation: 'Plausible' means believable or reasonable.

items.sentence: Please don't ___ / discriminate me with loads of technical terminology. Use plain, simple English!
items.explanation: 'Baffle' means to confuse someone.

items.sentence: It's not always easy to ___ / assess someone's suitability for a job just by talking to them in a short interview.
items.explanation: 'Assess' means to evaluate or judge the quality/suitability.

items.sentence: How can the government ___ / speculate spending so much on the armed forces?
items.explanation: 'Justify' means to provide a good reason for something.

items.sentence: Looking at the damage, I would ___ / deduce that the painting will cost at least €2,000 to restore properly.
items.explanation: 'Estimate' is used for guessing a cost or value.

items.sentence: I don't think you should ___ / presume that lots of people are going to help you with this project.
items.explanation: 'Presume' means to suppose that something is true without proof.

exercises.label: B
exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box. More than one word might fit in each gap, but try to use all the words.

items.sentence: It took me quite a long time to ___ what the aim of the game was.
items.explanation: 'Grasp' means to understand something difficult.

items.sentence: The jury are still ___ , but they're expected to reach a verdict soon.
items.explanation: A jury 'deliberates' when they discuss a case carefully.

items.sentence: I ___ from your tutor that you're thinking of changing course.
items.explanation: 'Gather' here means to understand or hear information indirectly.

items.sentence: You really have to ___ very hard when you are doing calligraphy.
items.explanation: Calligraphy requires intense focus or 'concentration'.

items.sentence: I'm seriously ___ asking for a pay rise tomorrow.
items.explanation: 'Considering' (or 'contemplating') means thinking about a future action.

items.sentence: How long do you ___ it'll take to get to Manchester?
items.explanation: 'Reckon' is an informal way to say 'think' or 'estimate'.

items.sentence: I don't ___ I could borrow your lecture notes this weekend, could I?
items.explanation: 'I don't suppose...' is a polite way to ask for something.

items.sentence: If you've ever ___ the question 'Why does the universe bother to exist?', then this is the book for you.
items.explanation: 'Ponder' means to think deeply about something.

items.sentence: Let's not even ___ the possibility of failure.
items.explanation: 'Contemplate' means to consider something as a possibility.

exercises.label: C
exercises.instruction: Each of the words in bold is in the wrong sentence. Write the correct words on the lines.

items.sentence: I'm in a real ___ because I've been invited to two parties on Saturday and I don't know which one to go to. (Original: hunch)
items.explanation: A 'dilemma' is a difficult choice between two options.

items.sentence: Dave said he's got complete ___ in us, and thinks we're going to do a great job. (Original: guesswork)
items.explanation: You have 'faith' (trust) in someone.

items.sentence: Sarah's a/an ___ when it comes to astrophysics, but she's not very practical in everyday things. (Original: intuition)
items.explanation: A 'genius' is someone highly talented or intelligent.

items.sentence: You often need to use a little educated ___ when you're starting a cryptic crossword. (Original: notion)
items.explanation: 'Educated guesswork' is a common collocation.

items.sentence: I've got a/an ___ that someone in this office is going to get promoted soon. (Original: ideology)
items.explanation: A 'hunch' is a feeling that something will happen.

items.sentence: Communism and capitalism are very different types of ___. (Original: dilemma)
items.explanation: Ideologies are sets of political beliefs.

items.sentence: Who gave you the ___ for the main character in the book? (Original: faith)
items.explanation: 'Inspiration' refers to the source of a creative idea.

items.sentence: Detective Tacker had absolutely no evidence that Roddard was involved, but all his ___ told him that he was. (Original: paradox)
items.explanation: 'Intuition' is a gut feeling or instinct.

items.sentence: I've got a small ___ about the exam syllabus. Should I talk to Mrs Jacobs about it? (Original: inspiration)
items.explanation: A 'query' is a question or doubt.

items.sentence: Paying mothers a salary to raise their children is an interesting ___. (Original: genius)
items.explanation: A 'notion' is an idea or concept.

items.sentence: The fact that more people would come here if the place wasn't so crowded is a real ___. (Original: query)
items.explanation: A 'paradox' is a contradictory situation.

items.sentence: the secondary education system also needs a major overhaul. firstly, the system only views the weakest learners as having special (1) ___.
items.explanation: 'Special needs' is a standard term in education.

items.sentence: The brightest and most (2) ___ students are not encouraged to develop to their full potential.
items.explanation: 'Conscientious' describes hardworking and diligent students.

items.sentence: My fifteen-year-old daughter, for example, has just spent the last month or so (3) ___ for exams.
items.explanation: 'Cramming' means studying hard just before exams.

items.sentence: They're just (4) ___ exams.
items.explanation: 'Mock exams' are practice exams.

items.sentence: Is the work she's been doing really going to make her more (5) ___ about her subjects...
items.explanation: 'Knowledgeable' means knowing a lot about a subject.

items.sentence: Thirdly, the standard (6) ___ doesn't give students any...
items.explanation: 'Curriculum' refers to the overall program of study.

items.sentence: ...doesn't give students any (7) ___ in developing practical work-related, living and social skills,
items.explanation: 'Tuition' refers to the teaching or instruction provided.

items.sentence: ...the difference is between (8) ___ someone else's work and making good use of someone else's ideas?
items.explanation: 'Plagiarising' is copying someone else's work.

items.sentence: How many of them are really able to go about (9) ___ - a skill that's essential at university...
items.explanation: 'Self-study' is independent learning.

items.sentence: Indeed, how many students (10) ___ from university totally unable to spell...
items.explanation: Students 'graduate' from university.

items.sentence: I'm going to have to mull this ___
items.answer: over for a while before I make a final decision.
items.explanation: 'Mull over' means to think carefully.

items.sentence: Detectives are still trying to piece ___
items.answer: together exactly what happened that evening.
items.explanation: 'Piece together' means to reconstruct a situation from information.

items.sentence: It took him a very long time to take ___
items.answer: in the fact that he'd never see her again.
items.explanation: 'Take in' means to understand or accept something.

items.sentence: Tell us how you hit ___
items.answer: upon such a great idea for a website.
items.explanation: 'Hit upon' means to suddenly have an idea.

items.sentence: It took us ages to figure ___
items.answer: out how to get everyone to the sports centre without Phil knowing.
items.explanation: 'Figure out' means to solve a problem or understand something.

items.sentence: I've just thought ___
items.answer: up a brilliant excuse to tell Rachel.
items.explanation: 'Thought up' means to invent or imagine.

items.sentence: I'm not sure we've thought ___
items.explanation: 'Thought through' means to consider facts thoroughly.

exercises.label: F
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap so that each second sentence has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence: I need to ___ over your offer very carefully before I make a decision.
items.explanation: mull over = consider carefully

items.sentence: Lee came ___ ___ the brilliant idea of booking a clown for the end-of-term party.
items.explanation: come up with = think of an idea

items.sentence: It's time you faced ___ ___ the fact that you've got responsibilities now.
items.explanation: face up to = accept and deal with

items.sentence: I'm going to have to brush ___ ___ my spoken German before I go to Munich.
items.explanation: brush up on = improve skills/knowledge

items.sentence: I didn't like the idea at first but I've ___ round ___ it now.
items.explanation: come round to = change opinion to agree

items.sentence: There's nothing to swot ___ ___ as it's a general knowledge quiz.
items.explanation: swot up on = study hard for an exam

items.sentence: I was unable to ___ out what the speaker was saying because we were right at the back of the hall.
items.explanation: make out = hear or understand with difficulty

items.sentence: We finally puzzled ___ a way to get the band into the hotel without the press knowing.
items.explanation: puzzle out = solve a complicated problem

items.sentence: I've been ___ up on the history of English language teaching recently.
items.explanation: read up on = get information by reading

exercises.label: G
exercises.instruction: Phrasal verbs with out, such as puzzle out, are often connected to the idea of finding information. Which of these phrasal verbs with out are also connected to this idea?

items.sentence: Did you find out what time the parents' meeting is?
items.explanation: find out = discover information

items.sentence: I've been checking out whether it's better to go by ferry or plane.
items.explanation: check out = investigate/examine for info

items.sentence: Susie reckons she's sussed out a great way to get into the club for free.
items.explanation: suss out = discover/realize something

items.sentence: We all piled out into the playground.
items.explanation: pile out = move in a group (physical movement)

items.sentence: I can't figure out how to change my password.
items.explanation: figure out = solve/understand

items.sentence: The band were completely drowned out by all the screaming fans.
items.explanation: drown out = be louder than another sound

items.sentence: I'm going to sound Bob out about his investing in the company.
items.explanation: sound out = try to find out someone's opinion

items.sentence: Two essays really do stand out from the rest.
items.explanation: stand out = be very noticeable

items.sentence: Jan's good at keeping secrets, so you'll have to worm it out of her!
items.explanation: worm out of = get information someone doesn't want to give

items.sentence: None of us could fathom out why the experiment wasn't working.
items.explanation: fathom out = understand/work out

exercises.label: H
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Jim seems to be ___ the misapprehension that tomorrow's a holiday. It isn't.
items.explanation: under the misapprehension that = believing something wrong

items.sentence: These findings cast doubt ___ previous research done in this field.
items.explanation: cast doubt on = make something seem uncertain

items.sentence: This photo's a little ___ of focus, but you can still see their faces.
items.explanation: out of focus = not clear

items.sentence: Look ___ the bright side - it's nearly the end of term.
items.explanation: look on the bright side = be optimistic

items.sentence: I'm not against animal testing ___ principle.
items.explanation: in principle = in general/theory

items.sentence: I think I'll always associate this song ___ our holiday in Majorca.
items.explanation: associate something with

items.sentence: You have to bear ___ mind that there'll be quite a few children there.
items.explanation: bear in mind = remember/consider

items.sentence: Where did Susan's belief ___ reincarnation come from?
items.explanation: belief in something

items.sentence: Dad said that my borrowing the car tonight was ___ of the question.
items.explanation: out of the question = impossible/not allowed

items.sentence: Hearing the news about Caroline put my small problems ___ perspective.
items.explanation: put into perspective = see things in their true importance

items.sentence: What's the best course of action from your point ___ view?
items.explanation: point of view = perspective

items.sentence: You were hired ___ the basis that you would be able to work on Sundays.
items.explanation: on the basis that = because/given that

items.sentence: ___ balance, I think the government's doing a reasonable job.
items.explanation: on balance = overall

items.sentence: We haven't taken Julie's thoughts ___ account at all.
items.explanation: take into account = consider

items.sentence: ___ conclusion, I'd like to thank everyone who supported me during the early years.
items.explanation: in conclusion = finally

exercises.label: I
exercises.instruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.sentence: I was so tired that I couldn't even think ___. / I'll come ___ to the point - you're fired! / It's time to set the record ___ about what really happened that night.
items.explanation: think straight, come straight to the point, set the record straight.

items.sentence: She's only eight, but she has the ___ age of an 18-year-old. / Many ___ illnesses can be cured these days. / I'm not very good at ___ arithmetic.
items.explanation: mental age, mental illness, mental arithmetic.

items.sentence: Philip does a very funny ___ of Tony. / You really made an ___ on Pete - he hasn't stopped talking about you! / Why was I under the ___ that the exam was on Thursday?
items.explanation: do an impression (imitate), make an impression, under the impression (believing).

items.sentence: Carter was the ___ behind the whole criminal operation. / Could I just pick your ___ about good places to eat around here? / We sat there racking our ___ trying to think of a place to take Paul and Doug in the evening.
items.explanation: brains behind (leader), pick your brains, rack your brains.

items.sentence: Someone with no ___ of smell can't taste food well either. / I can't make ___ of this film at all - what's going on? / Unplugging a toaster before you try to fix it is just common ___ , isn't it?
items.explanation: sense of smell, make sense (understand), common sense.

items.sentence: I thought Gordon's new invention wouldn't work at all, but in fact it worked like a ___. / Winning a cruise on the Queen Mary was a ___ come true for me and Albert. / I've had the same recurring ___ for four nights in a row.
items.explanation: worked like a dream, dream come true, recurring dream.

items.sentence: Motorists should always show ___ for pedestrians crossing the street at a zebra crossing. / I'll give some ___ to your proposals and get back to you. / We stupidly didn't take changes in interest rates into ___ when we got a mortgage for our house.
items.explanation: show consideration, give consideration, take into consideration.

exercises.label: J
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: It's (1) ___ my principles to trust anything I read on the Internet.
items.explanation: against one's principles = contrary to beliefs

items.sentence: I've started this organisation (2) ___ the belief that there are millions like me...
items.explanation: in the belief that = because one believes

items.sentence: ...who (3) ___ their doubts about information presented as fact...
items.explanation: have doubts = be unsure

items.sentence: There's no question (4) ___ us shutting down the Internet...
items.explanation: no question of = no possibility of

items.sentence: ...but DTOI has been set up with a (5) ___ to raising awareness.
items.explanation: with a view to doing = with the intention of

items.sentence: I made up my (6) ___ long ago not to completely trust anything I heard or read.
items.explanation: make up one's mind = decide

exercises.label: K
exercises.instruction: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence: I never thought that we'd have legal problems. crossed -> It ___ that we'd have legal problems.
items.explanation: cross one's mind = think of something

items.sentence: I've decided that teaching is not the right profession for me. conclusion -> I've ___ that teaching is not the right profession for me.
items.explanation: come to the conclusion = decide after thinking

items.sentence: Could you tell me where you were last night, Mr Johnson? account -> Could you ___ your whereabouts last night, Mr Johnson?
items.explanation: give an account of = describe/explain

items.sentence: The journalist pretended that she was a parent of one of the children. false -> The journalist ___ that she was a parent of one of the children.
items.explanation: give a false impression = make people believe something not true

items.sentence: You have to use logic and lateral thinking in equal measure in this job. strike -> You have to ___ logic and lateral thinking in this job.
items.explanation: strike a balance between = find a middle ground

items.sentence: I didn't tell Angie because I didn't want to hurt Eddie's feelings. consideration -> I didn't tell Angie ___ Eddie.
items.explanation: out of consideration for = because of care for someone

exercises.label: L
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the box.

items.sentence: The name Alan Hickson rings a ___ , but I can't place him.
items.explanation: ring a bell = sound familiar

items.sentence: Stop wasting time splitting ___ and look at the big picture.
items.explanation: split hairs = argue over minor details

items.sentence: Ask Simon about the sales targets. He always knows what's ___ .
items.explanation: know what's what = know important facts

items.sentence: You've got to have your ___ about you to succeed in advertising.
items.explanation: have your wits about you = think quickly/sensibly

items.sentence: Don't let success go to your ___ , will you?
items.explanation: go to one's head = make one conceited

items.sentence: No one told us that Diana was leaving, but we all put two and ___ together.
items.explanation: put two and two together = deduce

items.sentence: I'm going to go round the ___ if they don't turn that music down soon.
items.explanation: go round the bend = go crazy

items.sentence: Let's take ___ of the situation before we make a final decision.
items.explanation: take stock of = evaluate the situation

items.sentence: How did they manage to keep me completely in the ___ about this for so long?
items.explanation: in the dark = not knowing/uninformed

items.sentence: The problem with Jane is that sometimes she can't see the ___ for the trees.
items.explanation: not see the wood for the trees = miss the big picture

items.sentence: How did Dan take so long? He's usually quicker on the ___ than that, isn't he?
items.explanation: quick on the uptake = quick to understand

items.sentence: Frankly, you're completely wrong and you haven't got a ___ to stand on.
items.explanation: not have a leg to stand on = no way to prove right

exercises.label: M
exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: There was quite a bit of ___ because the examiner handed out the wrong exam papers. (CONFUSE)
items.explanation: noun form

items.sentence: You're acting completely ___ . Just calm down and pull yourself together. (RATIONAL)
items.explanation: adverb form (negative)

items.sentence: Einstein's ___ went unnoticed at school. (BRILLIANT)
items.explanation: noun form

items.sentence: This is generally considered to be the ___ guide to Australia. (DEFINE)
items.explanation: adjective form meaning 'authoritative'

items.sentence: It's absolutely ___ why the council decided to close down the youth club. (EXPLAIN)
items.explanation: adjective form (negative)

items.sentence: I just stood there and listened in ___ as Ben told me the news. (BELIEVE)
items.explanation: noun form (negative)

items.sentence: Greg's ___ to say the least, so I wouldn't ask him to choose a hotel. (DECIDE)
items.explanation: adjective form (negative)

items.sentence: Many children create ___ friends to talk to. (IMAGINE)
items.explanation: adjective form

items.sentence: He was found not guilty by reason of ___ and so was sent to a mental institution... (SANE)
items.explanation: noun form (negative)

items.sentence: Your argument is ___ . Just because you want me to be a doctor doesn't mean I want to be one. (LOGIC)
items.explanation: adjective form (negative)

exercises.label: N
exercises.instruction: The prefix ii-, as in illogical, is often used to make a positive word negative. Which of the words in bold in the following sentences are negative forms of positive words?

items.sentence: Doctors' handwriting is usually illegible.
items.explanation: illegible = not legible

items.sentence: There's a beautiful illustration on page six.
items.explanation: illustration is a base noun

items.sentence: People who are illiterate may need some help filling in forms.
items.explanation: illiterate = not literate

items.sentence: She had an illustrious career as a local politician.
items.explanation: illustrious is a base adjective

items.sentence: It's a totally illiberal law that's just going to restrict our freedoms further.
items.explanation: illiberal = not liberal

items.sentence: Several senior politicians have resigned over the illegality of the war.
items.explanation: illegality = not legality

items.sentence: Illegitimate children were given very little social recognition in the past.
items.explanation: illegitimate = not legitimate

items.sentence: The magician didn't really cut his assistant in two - it was only an illusion.
items.explanation: illusion is a base noun

items.sentence: The lights illuminated the building beautifully at night.
items.explanation: illuminate is a base verb

exercises.label: O
exercises.instruction: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: The (1) ___ of 'rhetoric', or effective public speaking, dates back thousands of years. (CONCEIVE)
items.explanation: noun form

items.sentence: The underlying (2) ___ behind rhetoric is that how you present an argument... (ASSUME)
items.explanation: noun form

items.sentence: There is (3) ___ plenty of evidence to support this idea... (DOUBT)
items.explanation: adverb form

items.sentence: ...it's practically (4) ___ , for example, for a successful politician to be a poor communicator... (THINK)
items.explanation: adjective form (negative)

items.sentence: Certainly, it is often said of politicians that they talk complete (5) ___ (SENSE)
items.explanation: noun form (negative prefix)

items.sentence: ...but what they say they say with such (6) ___ that we tend to believe them... (CONVINCE)
items.explanation: noun form

items.sentence: On the other hand, (7) ___ and knowledge are of little value... (WISE)
items.explanation: noun form

items.sentence: It is the combination of clear (8) ___ , (REASON)
items.explanation: noun form (process of thinking)

items.sentence: sound (9) ___ and effective presentation... (JUDGE)
items.explanation: noun form

items.sentence: A true rhetorician should always come across as knowledgeable, and never as (10) ___ or ignorant. (OPINION)
items.explanation: adjective form meaning 'holding stubborn opinions'
